title: Asuka and Shinjis relationship in episodes 16 18
display_title: Asuka and Shinjis relationship in episodes 16 18
wikitext:
[]: Asuka and Shinji's relationship in episodes 16-18             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Mon May 20, 2013 6:09 pm This is one of the many tangents that was spawned by the now-locked Chit-Chat topic "Asuka is too good for Shinji?". My particular focus in this post is addressing the question of exactly why Asuka did not tell Shinji who the pilot of Unit 03 was, but seeing as this inevitably involves discussion of their relationship as a whole at this point in the series, I broadened the topic title to that. Bagheera wrote: It seems really odd to me to embrace a reading of a scene that you know to be contradicted by other scenes in the work. I can sort of see NemZ's take on things, particularly in the broadcast version (which he favors); a reflexive "wait, you don't know about that???" followed by "NVM, not my problem" both fits the broadcast version of the character and dovetails nicely with the hospital scene at the end of 16 (to say nothing of her "I can't smile today" line in the scene in question). But this silent treatment business just doesn't add up.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Mon May 20, 2013 9:41 pm Shinji and Asuka are indeed fighting in #18 when Kaji is looking after the two, and it's about a lot more than Toji and Eva-03. In fact, Asuka's motivations here are rather more complex than at first or second glance appears. Indeed, she is currently in the midst of a major falling out with Shinji, stemming from his less than enthusiastic reaction to her kiss in Episode #15. There are layers, and layers, and layers to this. Firstly, we have to remember that, from the very beginning, Asuka's own self worth as a pilot has been threatened and undermined by Shinji's seemingly effortless victories. Episode #08  SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon May 20, 2013 10:29 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: While Asuka is still clearly the superior pilot at this point, his slow creeping up the ranks clearly threatens her status as the world's finest.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue May 21, 2013 8:02 am NemZ wrote: I'm not sure that's actually the case. She has greater fine motor control, superior training and a better psych rate for most of the series, but results don't lie... Shinji is just much more effective in actual combat situations.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Tue May 21, 2013 8:07 am NemZ wrote: She may have still wanted his attention but there's no reason to believe it's she's sincere in these endevours.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue May 21, 2013 8:43 am ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: Asuka is trying with Shinji; She really is trying. I made a case long ago in the Shinji and Asuka thread about what Asuka was looking fro in Shinji, and the short answer is really: a peer worthy enough for her.

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Tue May 21, 2013 11:18 am There seems to be a downward spiral for Asuka starting with Episode 15. In Episode 15 Asuka seems to make a major (from her perspective, anyway) attempt to try and catch Shinji's attention. First, she ditches her older date and comes home to see him, and her first words are not her typical "Baka Shinji" criticisms but rather words of praise about his cello ability. Shortly after, Asuka learns that Misato is out drinking with Kaji; without anyone else around, she then initiates the kiss scene, which seems like a childish dare, but is very important to her and could possibly have been planned for some time. Unfortunately, Shinji's complete lack of attention to Asuka's romantic intentions causes things to go downhill fast: Episode 15 Kiss Scene  SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue May 21, 2013 12:21 pm Bagheera wrote: That would have held before EoE, but we know that's clearly not the case now. Shinji's more effective because how mother bails him out on a regular basis -- apart from his sortie against Zeruel he's displayed no real skill in the cockpit otherwise.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue May 21, 2013 12:47 pm NemZ wrote: He pulled off all the synchronized dance-battle nonsense in ep10 well enough, didn't he? Backflips and Rider Kicks and all that, right alongside her. Clearly he's not talentless, he just doesn't usually fuck around with all the showy stuff and gets right to sticking his boot in an angel's ass. And don't dismiss Zeruel, since in that encounter it sure looks like he was winning until the power kicked off on him while Asuka did jack and shit with her attempt.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue May 21, 2013 1:43 pm Bagheera wrote: Right, but then EoE. No matter her sync issues Asuka's piloting skills are (or should be) beyond question.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Tue May 21, 2013 2:41 pm Shinoyami65 wrote: She is probably unhappy that a "stooge" like Toji should get to pilot, as she sees piloting as a special privilege, and is also displeased that she has another "competitor" to contend with.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Tue May 21, 2013 3:09 pm NemZ wrote: I didn't say she wasn't good, just that claims of her superiority aren't well justified. In every case where we can clearly compare the two Shinji does at least as well as she does. Yes, she kicks the hell out of the MPEs that may or may not be trying very hard, but Shinji never fights any of them nor does he ever face multiple opponents at once so there's no point of comparison.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue May 21, 2013 3:52 pm Bagheera wrote: even if she was thinking long-term that still doesn't explain her falling apart like that. He MIGHT leave an impressive legacy, so she starts wrecking her room and driving herself insane? I don't think so. Only two things explain that sort of behavior: grief and frustration born of confusion. I think she'd pretty solidly in the latter camp.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed May 22, 2013 2:10 am NemZ wrote: I find it odd that I'm giving her more credit for long-term thinking than you are, but okay...

[]:             Quote Post by Shinoyami65 » Wed May 22, 2013 8:15 am Bagheera wrote: No argument here. But don't forget this takes place over the course of a month; I don't think rage alone explains her deterioration over that length of time. This is made plain in the DC, of course, with the scene at the beginning of 22' at the train station. But even in the broadcast version I think things are more ambiguous than you let on.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed May 22, 2013 8:53 am Shinoyami65 wrote: At times Asuka seems to recover but then falls into depression with increasingly greater frequency, culminating in her big breakdown after Episode 22 and 23. As I detailed above she seems fairly devastated after Episode 15; however, she continues to act like her normal self when at school in Episode 17, attempting to keep her facade up. She also confides some of insecurities about piloting with Rei, her "rival", in Episode 16, yet is shocked to find herself confiding in Shinji in Episode 22.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed May 22, 2013 11:13 am The month-long break itself is probably making it worse. She's stuck doing nothing but drills, probably with her scores dropping off a tick or two every day while being continually reminded that Shinji went well beyond her best records, and it's all probably just obvious busywork to keep her out of the way while everyone else is far more interested in recovering Shinji then tending to her. And that's even assuming she CAN do sych training... it's possible that she's got nothing to do at all while 02 is being fixed. Hell, even the angels don't seem interested if Shinji isn't available to fight them... and if they did would either 00 or 02 be in any shape to fight back? For a good long while there I immagine NERV was quite literally defenseless adding even more to the tension, especially since Asuka would probably blame the damage to both evas on her complete inability to even scratch Zeruel.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Wed May 22, 2013 5:16 pm Now hold on, are the posts above mine that are talking about the month-long break and seemingly relating that to Asuka's tantrum in episode 20 taking into account that Asuka's tantrum happens on like the second day of that month? Secondly (and I guess this is addressed to Bagheera ), I don't think the possibility that Asuka was withholding the information about Touji from Shinji in episode 18 primarily to be mean precludes the other layers of her feelings for him we've been discussing. I felt that Asuka was withholding the information about Touji to be mean to Shinji because of how she was feeling in that moment (which, as Kaji assessed and as the background audio suggests, was a couples-fighting scene), but that doesn't mean I don't think she also cared for him as evidenced by the ending of episode 16. I could even entertain the possibility that Asuka withheld the information for both reasons (and maybe NemZ's as well). People rarely do things for just a single reason, after all. Maybe she remembered that she was in the process of being cold to him, but one level below that she also didn't want to really hurt him, and maybe also decided that it would be too much trouble to tell him because then she'd have to deal with his reaction, and perhaps she even did such a typically Asuka thing as convince herself that she withheld the information for the first and last reasons while neglecting to even consider consciously that she was doing it for the second. But I definitely think it must be considered that on some level she was doing it to be mean to Shinji, if only because so much else in that scene is pointing to it. It seems practically like the entire point of the scene within the context of the episode is to explain why Asuka didn't tell him. When you said "Layers upon layers, as OMF said, but let's look at it as a set of layers rather than picking one" I did a double-take because that's exactly what I felt that you were doing by dismissing Kaji's assessment of what was going on between Shinji and Asuka, and had even already written half a post (which eventually became this one) about the importance of working on multiple layers and not completely dismissing some of them (in this case, the more surface layers) when convenient. I don't think the "Asuka and Shinji are fighting in this scene (or at least Asuka is while Shinji remains clueless" layer should be dismissed.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed May 22, 2013 5:49 pm Monk Ed wrote: Secondly (and I guess this is addressed to Bagheera ), I don't think the possibility that Asuka was withholding the information about Touji from Shinji in episode 18 primarily to be mean precludes the other layers of her feelings for him we've been discussing. I felt that Asuka was withholding the information about Touji to be mean to Shinji because of how she was feeling in that moment (which, as Kaji assessed and as the background audio suggests, was a couples-fighting scene), but that doesn't mean I don't think she also cared for him as evidenced by the ending of episode 16.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Wed May 22, 2013 7:10 pm Bagheera wrote: No, between her surprise, her bit at the end, the fact she was disappointed instead of raging when talking to Hikari, and the fact that the end of 16 and their scene in 17 were still carrying on with the action arc charade (despite signs of fracture with the kiss and the sync tests in 16) I can't get petty meanness out of that scene at all. A selfish girl who sidesteps the issue because she doesn't want to deal with it? Check. Petty cruelty? Not so much.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Wed May 22, 2013 5:16 pm Now hold on, are the posts above mine that are talking about the month-long break and seemingly relating that to Asuka's tantrum in episode 20 taking into account that Asuka's tantrum happens on like the second day of that month? I felt that Asuka was withholding the information about Touji to be mean to Shinji because of how she was feeling in that moment (which, as Kaji assessed and as the background audio suggests, was a couples-fighting scene), but that doesn't mean I don't think she also cared for him as evidenced by the ending of episode 16. I could even entertain the possibility that Asuka withheld the information for both reasons (and maybe NemZ's as well). People rarely do things for just a single reason, after all. Maybe she remembered that she was in the process of being cold to him, but one level below that she also didn't want to really hurt him, and maybe also decided that it would be too much trouble to tell him because then she'd have to deal with his reaction, and perhaps she even did such a typically Asuka thing as convince herself that she withheld the information for the first and last reasons while neglecting to even consider consciously that she was doing it for the second. But I definitely think it

 with his reaction, and perhaps she even did such a typically Asuka thing as convince herself that she withheld the information for the first and last reasons while neglecting to even consider consciously that she was doing it for the second.
